message:
fix(tools): enable grep/find/ls by default and document 8 built-in tools (bd-21ztk)

The default --tools flag only included read, bash, edit, write, and
hashline_edit, even though grep, find, and ls were fully implemented
built-in tools. Users had to manually pass --tools to enable them.

Add grep, find, ls to the default tool set so all 8 built-in tools are
available out of the box. Update documentation and test assertions from
"7 built-in tools" to "8 built-in tools" across README.md, src/tools.rs,
tests/e2e_tools.rs, and tests/tools_hardened.rs. Update conformance
fixture and e2e CLI test to match the expanded default tools string.
Mark bd-21ztk as in-progress in the beads tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"bd-1it8r","title":"Package resource manifests should fail closed on invalid paths/types","description":"Fresh-eyes audit: package resource manifests currently only validate that [?25l is an object. Individual resource keys silently ignore malformed values (for example non-string arrays) and also accept paths that resolve outside the package root via absolute paths or  traversal. That can silently drop intended resources or load arbitrary outside-root paths. Make manifest resource keys validate as string-or-array-of-strings and reject any resolved path outside the package root.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-13T07:04:11.747681139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T07:04:22.171852690Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1iwi","title":"Workstream: Interactive Editor UX Parity (autocomplete, multiline, bang, images)","description":"# Goal
Bring the **interactive editor experience** to parity with legacy pi-mono, focusing on:
- Autocomplete (commands, templates, skills, file references, path completion)
- Multi-line editing behavior (Shift+Enter inserts newline, Enter submits)
- Bash shortcuts (`!` and `!!`)
- Clipboard image paste + drag/drop attachments

# Legacy Behavior (Source-of-Truth)
From `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/README.md` → “Editor”:
- File reference: type `@` to fuzzy-search project files
- Path completion: Tab to complete paths
- Multi-line: Shift+Enter (Ctrl+Enter on Windows Terminal)
- Images: Ctrl+V to paste, or drag onto terminal
- Bash commands: `!command` runs and sends output to LLM, `!!command` runs without sending

Legacy implementation details:
- Autocomplete is provided by `CombinedAutocompleteProvider` (slash commands, prompt templates, extension commands, skill commands, file path completion using `fd`).
- Image paste writes clipboard image to a temp file and inserts the file path into the editor.

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `src/interactive.rs` reserves Tab for future autocomplete but has no autocomplete UI.
- No `@` file fuzzy search.
- No Shift+Enter newline semantics (current behavior differs).
- No `!` / `!!` bash shortcuts.
- No clipboard image paste handler.

# Scope / Deliverables
## 1) Autocomplete engine
- Provide suggestions for:
  - Slash commands (built-in)
  - Prompt templates (`/<template>`)
  - Skill commands (`/skill:name`) when enabled
  - File references after typing `@` (fuzzy search project files)
  - Path completion when cursor is inside a path token

## 2) Autocomplete UI
- Render a dropdown/list with:
  - configurable max visible items (`autocomplete_max_visible`)
  - keyboard navigation
  - selection/accept behavior

## 3) Multi-line input semantics
- Shift+Enter inserts newline; Enter submits.
- Windows Terminal: Ctrl+Enter inserts newline.

## 4) Bash shortcuts
- `!cmd`: run and include output in LLM context.
- `!!cmd`: run but do not include output in LLM context.

## 5) Clipboard image paste and drag/drop
- Ctrl+V attempts to read clipboard image:
  - if an image exists, write it to a temp file and insert the file path at cursor.
  - do nothing (silent) if clipboard has no image or access denied.
- Drag/drop: terminals usually paste a file path; ensure we handle file paths cleanly.

# Testing
- Unit tests for autocomplete matching and insertion.
- State tests for key interactions (Tab, Shift+Enter, Ctrl+V).
- Integration tests for `!` / `!!` behavior (using deterministic command runner where possible).

# Dependencies
- Keybinding routing (`bd-3ip`) for Tab / Shift+Enter / Ctrl+V behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
[ ] Autocomplete exists (commands/templates/skills/files/paths)
[ ] Tab path completion works
[ ] @ fuzzy file reference works
[ ] Shift+Enter inserts newline; Enter submits
[ ] ! and !! bash shortcuts implemented
[ ] Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop attachment supported
[ ] Editor settings are wired (padding, max visible)
","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Autocomplete exists (commands/templates/skills/files/paths)
[ ] Tab path completion works
[ ] @ fuzzy file reference works
[ ] Shift+Enter inserts newline; Enter submits

[ ] Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop attachment supported
[ ] Editor settings are wired (padding, max visible)
[ ] Unit tests and integration tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this feature area
[ ] Integration/E2E scripts validate user-facing workflows and failure modes; emit detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9

[ ] Performance/UX budgets or evidence updated where applicable; regressions are detectable
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:38:54.183700146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T07:16:06.337384554Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T07:15:59.581234732Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1iwi","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1iwi","depends_on_id":"bd-3ip","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3220,"issue_id":"bd-1iwi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"All 10 children closed: autocomplete core + UI, multiline editing, bang shortcuts, image paste, file references, tab completion, styling, editor config, and tests. Workstream complete.","created_at":"2026-02-07T07:16:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1iy","title":"Migrate Gemini provider to asupersync HTTP","description":"# Migrate Gemini provider to asupersync HTTP

## Goal
Replace reqwest usage in src/providers/gemini.rs with asupersync HTTP client.

## Background
Gemini provider (Google Generative AI):
- generateContent endpoint with streaming
- Function declarations (tools)
- 631 lines, 4 unit tests

## Gemini-Specific Considerations
1. **Streaming URL**: Uses ?alt=sse query parameter
2. **Response Format**: Different JSON structure from Anthropic/OpenAI
3. **Tool Format**: functionDeclarations in tools array
4. **Content Parts**: text, functionCall, functionResponse

## Dependencies
- bd-9sa, bd-pwz, bd-37l (follow Anthropic pattern)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No reqwest imports
- [ ] 4 tests pass
- [ ] Streaming works","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CodexGPT52","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:08.130271194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:54.290956177Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T18:26:47.262441120Z","close_reason":"Already migrated to asupersync HTTP + SSE","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1iy","depends_on_id":"bd-37l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:55Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1iy","depends_on_id":"bd-gi3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:55Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1iy","depends_on_id":"bd-pwz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:55Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2156,"issue_id":"bd-1iy","author":"CodexGPT52","text":"Heads-up: src/providers/gemini.rs already uses asupersync HTTP client (src/http/client.rs) + src/sse.rs. Once bd-37l is closed (it was blocking this), I plan to close bd-1iy as already complete.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:20:44Z"},{"id":2157,"issue_id":"bd-1iy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verified bd-1iy is already complete: src/providers/gemini.rs uses asupersync HTTP client (src/http/client.rs) + canonical SSE parser (src/sse.rs). Closing now that bd-37l is closed.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:26:41Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1iz5","title":"Error UX: define hint taxonomy + mapping (Error -> hints)","description":"# Goal
Define the canonical mapping from internal error variants to user-facing remediation hints.

# Scope
- Enumerate the main error variants surfaced to users (config/auth/provider/tools/session/resources).
- For each variant, define:
  - a 1-line summary (human readable)
  - 0-2 actionable hints (commands, env vars, paths)
  - any contextual fields to print (file path, provider name, status code)

# Notes / Guidance
- Prefer stable phrasing so tests can assert on it.
- Prefer OS-specific hints only when we can detect OS reliably.
- Avoid suggesting destructive actions.

# Deliverable
- A documented table (in code or in `docs/troubleshooting.md`) that future contributors can extend.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mapping covers the top ~20 user-facing failure modes.
- [ ] Each hint is actionable and specific.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)

[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:23:01.683621713Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:28:58.192622969Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T21:35:53.512488787Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1iz5","depends_on_id":"bd-3am2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:08Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3284,"issue_id":"bd-1iz5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: Created src/error_hints.rs with ErrorHint struct mapping Error variants to user-facing hints. Covers 20+ failure modes with actionable remediation. 16 unit tests passing.","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:35:45Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-21nj4","title":"[SEC-2.4] Quarantine-to-trust promotion workflow for unknown/high-risk extensions","description":"## Background
Not all extensions should execute immediately after install; trust must be staged.

## Scope
- Add quarantine state for unsigned/high-risk/unknown packages.
- Define explicit promotion workflow with user acknowledgment and policy constraints.
- Ensure quarantined extensions cannot bypass policy via indirect registration paths.

## Deliverables
- State machine for trust lifecycle: quarantined -> restricted -> trusted.
- UX + CLI hooks for promotion and rollback to quarantine.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Quarantined extensions cannot execute dangerous hostcalls.
- [ ] Promotion requires explicit operator action with audit trail.
- [ ] Demotion back to quarantine is supported and tested.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description is implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests added/updated for success, failure, edge cases, and determinism where applicable
[ ] E2E scripts added/updated for benign flow, adversarial flow, and rollback/recovery flow relevant to this bead
[ ] E2E runs emit structured JSONL logs with: timestamp, issue_id, extension_id, capability, policy_profile, score, reason_codes, action, latency_ms, correlation_id, and redaction_summary
[ ] Artifact manifest is deterministic and linked in bead comments (paths + checksums/identifiers)
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test (plus targeted conformance/security suites)
[ ] If runtime behavior changed, docs/config examples are updated; if no behavior changed, explicitly record N/A rationale for e2e changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:39.786483189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T09:03:49.692880818Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T09:03:49.692782466Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["quarantine","security","supply-chain"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21nj4","depends_on_id":"bd-21vng","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-21nj4","depends_on_id":"bd-3br2a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2875,"issue_id":"bd-21nj4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Opus agent claiming bd-21nj4. This bead was just unblocked by completing its two dependencies: bd-21vng (install-time scanner) and bd-3br2a (lockfile provenance). Will implement quarantine state machine, trust promotion workflow with audit trail, and demotion support.","created_at":"2026-02-14T08:29:11Z"},{"id":2876,"issue_id":"bd-21nj4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed SEC-2.4. Delivered:
1. ExtensionTrustState enum (Quarantined/Restricted/Trusted) with strict state machine
2. ExtensionTrustTracker with promote/demote methods, operator ack requirement, risk score thresholds
3. TrustTransitionEvent audit trail with JSONL export (pi.ext.trust_lifecycle.v1)
4. is_hostcall_allowed_for_trust() function gating hostcall categories by trust state
5. initial_trust_state() deriving initial state from InstallTimeRiskReport
6. Risk score thresholds: Restricted requires >= 30, Trusted requires >= 50
7. 47 integration tests covering: state machine transitions, operator ack, risk thresholds, demotion, full lifecycle, audit JSONL, hostcall gating, auto-quarantine from risk report
All 136 integration tests + 134 lib tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-14T09:03:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-21vng","title":"[SEC-2.3] Install-time static scanner with deterministic risk classifier","description":"## Background
We need early detection of suspicious extension patterns before first execution.

## Scope
- Expand static scanning for dangerous APIs/patterns and privilege-seeking behavior.
- Produce deterministic risk tiers with explainable rule hits.
- Write evidence ledger entries that can be correlated with runtime behavior later.

## Deliverables
- Scanner rulebook + tests.
- Risk classification report format consumed by policy/quarantine flows.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scanner output is deterministic and reproducible.
- [ ] Each risk finding includes rule ID, rationale, and code location.
- [ ] Rule updates are versioned and backward-auditable.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description is implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests added/updated for success, failure, edge cases, and determinism where applicable
[ ] E2E scripts added/updated for benign flow, adversarial flow, and rollback/recovery flow relevant to this bead
[ ] E2E runs emit structured JSONL logs with: timestamp, issue_id, extension_id, capability, policy_profile, score, reason_codes, action, latency_ms, correlation_id, and redaction_summary
[ ] Artifact manifest is deterministic and linked in bead comments (paths + checksums/identifiers)
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test (plus targeted conformance/security suites)
[ ] If runtime behavior changed, docs/config examples are updated; if no behavior changed, explicitly record N/A rationale for e2e changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:39.547553786Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T09:17:57.478720742Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T08:04:15.893580564Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["scanner","security","supply-chain"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21vng","depends_on_id":"bd-2nr0q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-21vng","depends_on_id":"bd-3jpm3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-21vng","depends_on_id":"bd-f0huc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2394,"issue_id":"bd-21vng","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TopazFalcon claiming bd-21vng. Plan: implement install-time static scanner with deterministic risk classifier for extension code. Will scan for dangerous APIs/patterns, produce deterministic risk tiers, and write evidence ledger entries. Target files: src/extension_preflight.rs and possibly new scanner module.","created_at":"2026-02-14T07:43:25Z"},{"id":2395,"issue_id":"bd-21vng","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claude Opus 4.6 agent claiming bd-21vng. Will implement deterministic risk classifier that synthesizes existing PreflightAnalyzer + CompatibilityScanner signals into a single reproducible risk verdict. Deliverables: scanner rulebook, risk classification report format, deterministic scoring algorithm, and comprehensive tests.","created_at":"2026-02-14T07:45:57Z"},{"id":2396,"issue_id":"bd-21vng","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed SEC-2.3. Implemented:
1. Composite risk classifier (InstallTimeRiskReport) combining PreflightReport + SecurityScanReport signals
2. InstallRecommendation enum (Allow/Review/Block) with deterministic decision tree
3. classify_extension_source() and classify_extension_path() convenience functions
4. Fixed pre-existing contains_timer_abuse() bug (missing semicolon trimming)
5. Created tests/install_time_security_scanner.rs with 35+ integration tests covering all 17 security rules, false positive avoidance, determinism, JSON roundtrip, multi-file scanning, evidence ledger
6. All 148 integration tests + 99 lib tests + 115 e2e_workflow tests pass","created_at":"2026-02-14T08:04:05Z"},{"id":2397,"issue_id":"bd-21vng","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TopazFalcon: Added 9 new security rules (SEC-SPAWN-001, SEC-CONSTRUCTOR-001, SEC-NATIVEMOD-001, SEC-GLOBAL-001, SEC-SYMLINK-001, SEC-CHMOD-001, SEC-SOCKET-001, SEC-WASM-001, SEC-ARGUMENTS-001). Bumped rulebook to v2.0.0. Added deterministic multi-key sort (tier+file+line+column+rule). Added ~50 tests for new rules + determinism proofs. All 134 unit + 7 external + 115 e2e tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-14T09:17:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-21wy6","title":"Honor prerelease semantics in version update checks","description":"Fresh-eyes audit: src/version_check.rs strips prerelease identifiers before comparing versions, so a stable release like 1.2.3 is treated as equal to a prerelease current build like 1.2.3-beta.1. That suppresses update notifications for prerelease users. Switch is_newer() to real semver ordering and add focused regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:24:46.001568413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:45:36.116417350Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T01:45:36.116394818Z","close_reason":"Fixed in src/version_check.rs: version comparisons now preserve prerelease ordering, ignore build metadata, and treat malformed cached versions as Failed instead of UpToDate; regression coverage added.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-21ztk","title":"Built-in tool count/docs should reflect hashline_edit exposure","description":"The project still claims "7 built-in tools" in README.md, src/tools.rs module docs, and multiple tests, but the runtime/tool registry exposes hashline_edit as a normal tool. Evidence: src/agent.rs emits every registry tool into provider ToolDef values, src/sdk.rs test asserts 8 built-in tools, and system-prompt/tooling tests mention hashline_edit explicitly. We need to resolve the inconsistency by either documenting 8 built-in tools (and updating affected tests/docs) or deliberately hiding/removing hashline_edit from the advertised built-in surface.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-13T06:08:09.245163529Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T06:08:09.245163529Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-229m","title":"Feature: Compatibility gap analysis for expanded corpus","description":"Map expanded extensions to required shims/hostcalls; produce a prioritized gap matrix for unmodified compatibility.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:00:40.853214612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:37:32.727581739Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:37:32.321979646Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","compatibility","extensions"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-229m","depends_on_id":"bd-po15","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2388,"issue_id":"bd-229m","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Purpose
Translate the expanded corpus into a concrete compatibility gap matrix. The goal is a prioritized, actionable list of shims/hostcalls required for unmodified compatibility.

Guiding Principles
- No per-extension exceptions: all compatibility gaps map to general-purpose shims or hostcalls.
- Prioritize Tier-1 coverage and high-frequency Node APIs (fs, path, child_process, process, Buffer, URL, crypto).
- Keep performance + determinism constraints from EXTENSIONS.md.

Outputs
- A gap matrix by extension + API.
- A prioritized list of shim tasks, linked to existing workstreams where possible.

 {"id":"bd-229v","title":"E2E: Extension Registration lifecycle with detailed JSONL logging","description":"End-to-end test script for extension registration APIs with comprehensive logging.

## Scenario: Full Registration Lifecycle

1. Load test extension that registers:
   - 2 slash commands (/ext-hello, /ext-echo)
   - 1 keyboard shortcut (Ctrl+E)
   - 1 CLI flag (--ext-verbose)
   - 1 custom provider (mock-provider)

2. Verify registrations:
   - /ext-hello appears in autocomplete
   - /ext-hello executes JS handler
   - Shortcut triggers handler
   - --ext-verbose parsed from CLI
   - mock-provider in model list

## Logging Requirements

### JSONL Format
Each line:
```json
{"ts": "...", "level": "DEBUG", "component": "extension", "event": "...", "data": {...}}
```

### Required Events to Log
- extension_load_start
- hostcall_register_command{name, description}
- hostcall_register_shortcut{key, description}
- hostcall_register_flag{name, type}
- hostcall_register_provider{name, api}
- extension_load_complete{tools, commands, shortcuts, flags, providers}
- command_execute_start{name, args}
- command_execute_complete{name, result}

### Artifact Output
- logs/e2e_registration_{timestamp}.jsonl
- Screenshots for TUI verification (optional)

## Test Script Location

 {"id":"bd-3lts","title":"Conformance: Tier 4 - Multi-capability official extensions (12 exts)","description":"# Conformance: Tier 4 - Multi-capability official extensions (12 exts)

## Extensions
bookmark, claude-rules, custom-compaction, event-bus, handoff, notify, todo, mac-system-theme, shutdown-command, dynamic-resources, rpc-demo

## What These Test
These extensions use MULTIPLE API surfaces simultaneously:
- registerTool + on() event handlers
- registerCommand + registerShortcut
- registerFlag + getFlag
- registerProvider (custom model providers)
- registerMessageRenderer (custom UI for messages)

## Complexity
These are significantly more complex than Tiers 1-3. They test:
- Interaction between registration types
- State management across capabilities
- Complex event handler chains
- Flag-dependent behavior

## Mock Requirements
- Full session mock (messages, name, model)
- Exec mocks (some extensions run commands)
- UI mock (notifications, dialogs)
- EventBus mock (event-bus extension)

## Key Extensions
- bookmark: registerCommand + session access
- notify: registerTool + exec (system notifications)
- todo: registerTool + registerCommand + session state
- custom-compaction: subscribes to session_before_compact + modifies compaction
- handoff: registerCommand + registerTool + complex session management
- dynamic-resources: multi-file, registers dynamic resource discovery

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 12 extensions pass registration comparison
- 95%+ pass rate (1 allowed failure for platform-specific like mac-system-theme)
- All failures documented with root cause","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:23:12.299091282Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:55:04.306286033Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:55:04.306129882Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lts","depends_on_id":"bd-150s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:15Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lts","depends_on_id":"bd-21dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:15Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3lv3","title":"Implement pi.events for inter-extension communication","description":"Allow extensions to communicate via a shared event bus.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
const eventBus = pi.events;

// Emit custom event
eventBus.emit(eventName: string, data: unknown): void

// Listen for events
const unsubscribe = eventBus.on(eventName: string, handler: (data) => void): () => void
```

## Use Cases
- Extension coordination (one extension signals another)
- Workflow automation (chain of extensions)
- State sharing between extensions

## Implementation Requirements
1. SharedEventBus in extension runtime
2. Hostcall handlers for 'events.emit' and 'events.on'
3. Handler registration with unsubscribe support
4. Event dispatch to all registered handlers

## Concurrency Notes
- Multiple handlers may fire concurrently
- Handlers execute in JS runtime context
- Errors in one handler shouldn't affect others

## Test Plan
- Unit test: emit triggers registered handlers
- Unit test: unsubscribe stops handler calls
- Unit test: multiple handlers receive same event
- Integration test: two extensions communicate

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (SharedEventBus)
- src/extensions_js.rs (hostcall handlers)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:11:54.533323213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:20:11.087167036Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:20:11.087100983Z","close_reason":"Fully implemented: JS-side __pi_events_native in extensions_js.rs (emit/list ops), Rust-side dispatch_events in extension_dispatcher.rs:417-493 handles list and emit, event handlers tracked in __pi_hook_index.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lv3","depends_on_id":"bd-2ca4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lv3","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3m7f","title":"Workstream: Documentation Parity (core user docs)","description":"# Goal
Port and adapt the **core user documentation** from legacy pi-mono into this Rust repo so that:
- Users can install/configure/run Pi without reading legacy docs.
- Interactive commands and keybindings are documented accurately.
- Session/tree semantics are clear and match implementation.

# Background
Legacy docs live in:
`legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/*`

This repo currently has strong extension documentation and test matrices, but is missing most end-user docs (settings, keybindings, sessions, tree navigation, TUI, providers, packages, etc.).

# Scope
Create/port the following docs into `docs/` (Rust-adapted):
- `docs/keybindings.md`
- `docs/settings.md`
- `docs/session.md`
- `docs/tree.md`
- `docs/tui.md`
- `docs/themes.md`
- `docs/rpc.md`
- `docs/providers.md`
- `docs/models.md`
- `docs/skills.md`
- `docs/prompt-templates.md`
- `docs/packages.md`
- `docs/terminal-setup.md`
- `docs/development.md`
- `docs/windows.md`
- `docs/termux.md`

# Guidelines
- Docs must reflect **Rust implementation reality** (flags, paths, defaults) while preserving legacy intent.
- Where behavior is not yet implemented, mark clearly and link to the bead ID that tracks it.

- [ ] All interactive commands have doc coverage.

 {"id":"bd-3ml","title":"Implement verbose test logging infrastructure","description":"# Implement verbose test logging infrastructure

## Goal
Create shared infrastructure for detailed logging in all integration tests,
enabling easy debugging when tests fail.

## Background
E2E and integration tests need detailed logging to debug failures:
- Every test action should be logged
- Timestamps for timing analysis
- Context (file paths, values) for reproduction
- Logs dumped automatically on test failure

## Implementation

### Log Infrastructure
```rust
// tests/common/logging.rs

use std::sync::Mutex;
use std::time::Instant;

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct LogEntry {
    pub timestamp: Instant,
    pub level: LogLevel,
    pub category: &'static str,
    pub message: String,
    pub context: Vec<(String, String)>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum LogLevel {
    Debug,
    Info,
    Warn,
    Error,
}

pub struct TestLogger {
    entries: Mutex<Vec<LogEntry>>,
    start: Instant,
}
```

### Redaction + Artifacts
- Add helpers to redact sensitive values in env/header/context keys (API keys, auth headers).
- Add a simple artifact registry: `record_artifact(name, path)` so tests can attach outputs.
- On panic, dump logs + artifact list (paths) to stderr.

### Test Harness Integration
```rust
// tests/common/harness.rs

pub struct TestHarness {
    pub logger: TestLogger,
    pub temp_dir: TempDir,
}

impl Drop for TestHarness {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        if std::thread::panicking() {
            eprintln!("\n=== TEST LOGS ===\n{}", self.logger.dump());
            eprintln!("=== ARTIFACTS ===\n{}", self.logger.dump_artifacts());
        }
    }
}
```

### Optional File Output
- If `TEST_LOG_PATH` is set, write logs to that path in addition to stderr.

## Files
- tests/common/mod.rs
- tests/common/logging.rs
- tests/common/harness.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TestLogger captures all levels
- [ ] Context key-values supported
- [ ] Timestamps included
- [ ] Redaction helpers present
- [ ] Artifact registry present
- [ ] Auto-dump on test failure
- [ ] Optional file output works
- [ ] All integration tests use harness","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:54.070756084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:50.752577524Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T05:48:33.124430233Z","close_reason":"Implemented redaction helpers, artifact registry + dump, and TEST_LOG_PATH file output in test logging; harness now dumps artifacts on panic and provides record_artifact. Acceptance criteria satisfied.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ml","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:14Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3815,"issue_id":"bd-3ml","author":"QuietBear","text":"Reviewed existing tests/common logger/harness: core logging already present but missing redaction, artifact registry, and TEST_LOG_PATH dump.  {"id":"bd-3mml","title":"Auto-generate scenario skeletons from extension source analysis","description":"Build a tool that analyzes extension TypeScript source code and auto-generates scenario skeleton JSON files. For each extension, the tool should:

1. Parse registration calls to identify: tools (name, parameters), commands (name), event hooks (event names), providers
2. For each registered tool: generate a basic invocation scenario with plausible parameters
3. For each event hook: generate an event dispatch scenario with the expected event payload shape
4. For each command: generate a command invocation scenario
5. Mark all expected outputs as 'TODO: fill from reference capture'

This dramatically reduces the manual effort of writing 100+ scenario files. The skeletons can be enriched manually or via the TypeScript reference capture pipeline.

Input: extension .ts source files
Output: tests/ext_conformance/scenarios/<extension-id>.json per extension","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:13:08.599274115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:43:56.376080545Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:43:52.635622717Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mml","depends_on_id":"bd-1k8t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mml","depends_on_id":"bd-2vrw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:00Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2625,"issue_id":"bd-3mml","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting work.  {"id":"bd-3qm","title":"Keybindings: load ~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json + merge","description":"# Goal
Load user keybinding overrides from `~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json` and merge them over the default bindings.

# Required Behavior
- File location: `~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json` (legacy).
- JSON shape: object mapping actionId → string | string[]. Example:
  ```json
  {
    "cursorUp": ["up", "ctrl+p"],
    "cursorDown": ["down", "ctrl+n"],
    "deleteWordBackward": ["ctrl+w", "alt+backspace"]
  }
  ```
- User values override defaults for that action.
- Unknown actions: ignore with warning/diagnostic.
- Invalid key strings: ignore with warning/diagnostic.

# Implementation Notes
- Prefer loading once at startup, and allow reload via `/reload` in a later task.
- Thread-safety: interactive mode should hold an immutable snapshot of bindings.
- Diagnostics should be visible to the user (startup header warning or `/reload` status), but must not abort startup.

# Dependencies
- Needs the action catalog + parser.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Overrides take effect in interactive mode.
- [ ] Invalid config never crashes startup.
- [ ] Diagnostics are actionable (include file path and failing key/action).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)

[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented

[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"claude-opus","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:36:30.244375962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:16.502534521Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:00:18.928426465Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3qm","depends_on_id":"bd-103","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3qm","depends_on_id":"bd-3ip","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3qm","depends_on_id":"bd-cru","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:11Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3556,"issue_id":"bd-3qm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Keybindings user config loading implementation complete.

**Added to src/keybindings.rs:**

1. **KeyBindingsLoadResult struct:**
   - Holds loaded bindings + path + warnings
   - has_warnings() and format_warnings() helpers

2. **KeyBindingsWarning enum:**
   - ReadError - file read failure
   - ParseError - invalid JSON
   - UnknownAction - unrecognized action ID
   - InvalidKey - unparseable key string
   - InvalidKeyValue - wrong type (not string)

3. **KeyBindings methods:**
   - user_config_path() → ~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json
   - load_from_user_config() → loads with diagnostics
   - load_from_path_with_diagnostics() → full implementation

**Behavior:**
- Missing file returns defaults with no warnings (normal case)
- Invalid JSON returns defaults with ParseError warning
- Unknown actions logged and ignored
- Invalid keys logged and skipped (valid keys still applied)
- Never fails/crashes - always returns valid bindings

**Config format supported:**
```json
{
  "cursorUp": ["up", "ctrl+p"],
  "cursorDown": "down"
}
```
- Values can be string or array of strings
- Action IDs are camelCase (matching AppAction serde format)

**Tests added (7 new):**
- test_load_from_nonexistent_path_returns_defaults
- test_load_valid_override
- test_load_warns_on_unknown_action
- test_load_warns_on_invalid_key
- test_load_warns_on_invalid_json
- test_load_handles_invalid_value_type
- test_warning_display_format

Total: 39 keybindings tests passing.
